executive_summary.summary: The Plausible.io website has some accessibility and security issues, but overall it has a good performance and SEO score.

key_findings.title: Missing ARIA attributes
key_findings.description: ARIA attributes are not properly matched to their roles, which can make screen readers announce elements with generic names.
key_findings.recommendation: Ensure ARIA attributes match their roles to provide a better experience for screen reader users.

key_findings.title: Insufficient input field names
key_findings.description: Some input fields are missing accessible names, which can make screen readers announce input fields with generic names.
key_findings.recommendation: Provide accessible names for all input fields to ensure screen reader users can interact with them.

key_findings.title: Missing security headers
key_findings.description: Missing security headers can make the site vulnerable to various attacks, such as XSS and clickjacking.
key_findings.recommendation: Add the missing security headers to the site's configuration.

key_findings.title: Image elements do not have alt attributes
key_findings.description: Image elements do not have alt attributes, which can affect search engine understanding and accessibility.
key_findings.recommendation: Add alt attributes to image elements to improve accessibility and search engine understanding.

performance.summary: The page achieves a perfect performance score. No critical issues were found.

findings.evidence: LCP: 1.4 s mobile / 0.4 s desktop; budget 2.5 s. CLS: 0 mobile / 0 desktop; budget 0.1. INP: not reported.
findings.impact: The page meets all Core Web Vitals thresholds.
The page does not have any render-blocking resources.

findings.evidence: Estimated savings of 14 KiB from efficient cache lifetimes.
findings.impact: Improving cache lifetimes can reduce the page's load time.
findings.recommendation: Consider implementing efficient cache lifetimes to reduce the page's load time.

findings.evidence: The page has 660 elements in the DOM.
findings.impact: The page's DOM size is within acceptable limits.

accessibility.summary: Automated tooling detects roughly a third of WCAG failures; manual review is required for the categories listed in `manual_review_required`. The site has some accessibility issues, including missing ARIA attributes and insufficient input field names.

findings.evidence: ARIA attributes are not properly matched to their roles.
findings.impact: Screen readers may announce elements with generic names, making them unusable for users who rely on screen readers.
findings.recommendation: Ensure ARIA attributes match their roles to provide a better experience for screen reader users.

findings.evidence: Some input fields are missing accessible names.
findings.impact: Screen readers may announce input fields with generic names, making them unusable for users who rely on screen readers.
findings.recommendation: Provide accessible names for all input fields to ensure screen reader users can interact with them.

seo.summary: The page has a good SEO score, with some minor issues. The score is adjusted from 92 to 92 as there are no crawlability issues found.

findings.evidence: The page has a meta description, but it's not explicitly mentioned in the audit results.
findings.impact: Meta descriptions may be included in search results to concisely summarize page content.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding a meta description to the page to improve search engine results.

findings.evidence: Image elements do not have [alt] attributes.
findings.impact: Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute.
findings.recommendation: Add alt attributes to image elements to improve accessibility and search engine understanding.

security.summary: Plausible.io has a good security posture but lacks some security headers and has a few areas for improvement.

findings.evidence: TLS protocol is TLSv1.3, which is a good version, but it's not the latest version available.
findings.impact: Using an outdated TLS version may make the site vulnerable to known attacks.
findings.recommendation: Consider upgrading to the latest TLS version.

findings.evidence: Missing security headers: strict-transport-security, content-security-policy, x-frame-options, x-content-type-options, referrer-policy.
findings.impact: Missing security headers can make the site vulnerable to various attacks, such as XSS and clickjacking.
findings.recommendation: Add the missing security headers to the site's configuration.

findings.evidence: Present security header: permissions-policy with value 'interest-cohort=()'.
findings.impact: The permissions-policy header is present, but its value may not be optimal.
findings.recommendation: Consider reviewing the permissions-policy header value to ensure it's optimal for the site's needs.

findings.evidence: Sitemap.xml contains URLs that may be sensitive: https://plausible.io/data-policy.
findings.impact: Exposing sensitive URLs in the sitemap.xml can make the site vulnerable to information disclosure attacks.
findings.recommendation: Consider removing sensitive URLs from the sitemap.xml or restricting access to them.

findings.evidence: No vulnerable libraries were detected by Lighthouse.
findings.impact: The site may still have vulnerable libraries that are not detected by Lighthouse.
findings.recommendation: Consider using a more comprehensive vulnerability scanning tool to detect potential issues.
